privileged_info_summary: California has 58 counties. The task uses real EPA Air Quality System (AQS) PM2.5 monitoring site data (https://aqs.epa.gov/aqsweb/airdata/aqs_sites.zip) containing 1040 California monitors, and US Census TIGER 2022 county boundaries (https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/TIGER2022/COUNTY/tl_2022_us_county.zip). The agent must analyze PM2.5 monitoring coverage gaps by counting monitors per county, computing distance from unmonitored county centroids to nearest monitor, calculating monitoring density (monitors/km²), and producing enriched GeoJSON and CSV outputs with these computed fields. All spatial calculations must use projected CRS (EPSG:3310 California Albers) to ensure correct area and distance measurements.
